article_title: Git 2.55 Adds a 'git history fixup' Command, Brings the FSMonitor Daemon to Linux, and Lets git push Target Remote Groups

content_preview.title: Git 2.55 Adds a 'git history fixup' Command, Brings the FSMonitor Daemon to Linux, and Lets git push Target Remote Groups
content_preview.summary: Released June 29, the update extends the experimental git history command, adds inotify-based filesystem monitoring on Linux, and lets git push write to a group of remotes at once.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Git project released Git 2.55 on June 29, 2026, with features and bug fixes from over 100 contributors, 33 of them new, according to [GitHub's release highlights](https://github.blog/open-source/git/highlights-from-git-2-55/). The release extends the experimental `git history` command with a `fixup` subcommand, brings Git's built-in filesystem monitor daemon to Linux, and lets `git push` target a group of remotes using the same shorthand previously reserved for `git fetch`.

## ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (637 words, within the 400-1200 News range). The headline and Overview lead correctly center the three genuinely-new 2.55 features (git history fixup subcommand, Linux FSMonitor via inotify, git push to remote groups), not Rust. Inverted-pyramid structure with a sober 'What We Don't Know' section that resists over-claiming.
editor_notes.source_verification: All three source snapshots read from disk (gunzip) and verified. source-0.html.gz (github.blog, 200): confirms 'over 100 contributors, 33 of them new', the June 29 2026 date, the verbatim fixup quotes ('builds on the experimental git history command', 'applies the changes currently staged in the index to an earlier commit', 'folds it into the original recipe commit and replays the descendant commits on top'), 'only on macOS and Windows. Git 2.55 adds support for Linux, where the implementation uses inotify', the remote-groups quote with the remotes.publish 'github gitlab mirror' / git push publish main example, and the --graph-lane-limit '~' detail. source-1.html.gz (about.gitlab.com, 200): confirms verbatim 'takes the staged changes and amends them into the given commit', 'all other local branches that contain the fixed-up commit are updated as well', 'support for Linux has been added', 'inotify(7) is used', 'git-push(1) now accepts a remote group too', the Rust quote 'With this v2.55 release the Rust compiler is required unless you explicitly disable it in the build system', 'make NO_RUST=YesPlease', and the 'In November 2025, in Git 2.52, the first Rust production code was introduced into Git' / varint timeline. source-2.html.gz (linuxiac.com, 200): confirms 'new experimental git history fixup <commit> command, which allows users to apply staged changes directly to an earlier commit and replay subsequent commits on top', 'the most notable feature', and 'is expected to accelerate commands such as git status in large repositories'. Every direct quote is verbatim. No snapshot failures.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics trace to cited sources. Rule 9 satisfied: every 2.55 release specific is sourced to the GitHub Blog or GitLab engineering blog primary sources. Rust-by-default is correctly handled as CONTINUITY, not a new 2.55 claim — the only new 2.55 Rust fact ('compiler required unless explicitly disabled') is attributed verbatim to GitLab, while the 'default-enable Rust in both build systems in Git 2.53' statement is attributed to the prior Machine Herald Git 2.53 article via the 'as previously reported' internal cross-reference (which is accurate: that article states 'default-enable Rust support'). Both internal cross-references resolve to existing published articles: /article/2026-04/22-git-254-ships... and /article/2026-02/28-git-253-arrives... Phoronix (which 403s) is correctly NOT cited.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Quotes attributed to outlets; no editorializing or sensationalism; no AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Genuinely new. No prior Git 2.55 article exists in the archive. Distinct from other June Git-adjacent coverage (GitLab agent-native git, GitHub OIDC immutable IDs).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-sourced release write-up with an intact provenance chain (no orphan URLs, all three sources bidirectionally linked, both internal cross-references valid). Rule 9 and the Rust-continuity requirement are both satisfied. APPROVE.
